Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.saml

Class SamlAuthFilter

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.AuthFilter
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.saml.SamlAuthFilter
All Implemented Interfaces:
jakarta.servlet.Filter, Authenticator
public class SamlAuthFilter extends AuthFilter
AuthFilter that authenticates requests carrying a SAML 2.0 <samlp:Response> delivered via the SamlBinding.POST or SamlBinding.REDIRECT HTTP binding.

On each request this filter:

  1. Inspects the URL path against the configured consumerPath (default /saml/acs). If the request path does not match, returns Optional.empty() (filter does not apply).
  2. For the SamlBinding.POST binding: reads the SAMLResponse form parameter and base64-decodes it. For SamlBinding.REDIRECT: reads the SAMLResponse query parameter, base64-decodes it, and then DEFLATE-inflates (RFC 1951) per OASIS SAML 2.0 Redirect binding rules.
  3. Delegates to SamlAssertionValidator.validate(String). On success, builds an AuthResult carrying the resolved ClaimsPrincipal. On failure, re-throws as an AuthenticationException with a WWW-Authenticate: SAML ... challenge.
Roles

If the resolved ClaimsPrincipal contains a claim whose name equals the configured rolesClaim (default "roles") and whose value is a List of String, those role names are populated on the AuthResult. Otherwise the role set is empty.

Usage

var validator = SamlAssertionValidator.create() .metadataResolver(SamlMetadataResolvers.url("https://idp.example.com/metadata")) .spEntityId("https://sp.example.com") .expectedIssuer("https://idp.example.com") .build(); AuthFilterChain.create(bs) .append(SamlAuthFilter.create() .consumerPath("/saml/acs") .binding(SamlBinding.POST) .validator(validator) .build()) .build();

See Also:
  • SamlAssertionValidator
  • SamlBinding
  • SAML 2.0 Bindings (OASIS)
  • AuthN Filter Framework
Since:
10.0.0

    • authenticate

      public Optional<AuthResult> authenticate(jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest req) throws AuthenticationException
      Description copied from interface: Authenticator
      Inspects the request and returns the authentication result.
      Parameters:
      req - The incoming HTTP request. Never null.
      Returns:
      The authentication result, or Optional.empty() if this authenticator does not apply.
      Throws:
      AuthenticationException - If the request carries recognizable credentials that are invalid.
